explanation.rule: When an adjective ends in -ic or -al, the adverb is formed by adding -ally rather than just -ly.

groups.note: Used with adjectives ending in -ic (e.g. magic → magically) or -al (e.g. gradual → gradually). Note: public → publicly is an exception.

examples.definition: in the most essential or fundamental way
in a very sudden or impressive way
in a way that follows clear reasoning
in a way that is usual or expected
relating to the body or material things

wordForms.definition: in the most essential or fundamental way
wordForms.example: The two proposals are basically the same.
wordForms.rootDefinition: forming the most important or essential part

wordForms.definition: in a very sudden, great, or impressive way
wordForms.example: Sales increased dramatically after the advertisement.
wordForms.rootDefinition: sudden and striking; relating to drama

wordForms.definition: in a way related to the economy or finances
wordForms.example: The country was still economically unstable after the crisis.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to the economy or financial matters

wordForms.definition: in a way relating to feelings and emotions
wordForms.example: The film was emotionally powerful and left many people in tears.

wordForms.definition: slowly, in small stages over time
wordForms.example: She gradually recovered her confidence after the setback.
wordForms.rootDefinition: happening slowly over a period of time

wordForms.definition: as if by magic; in a wonderful or surprising way
wordForms.example: The problem seemed to magically disappear overnight.
wordForms.rootDefinition: having special powers; relating to magic

wordForms.definition: relating to the body rather than the mind
wordForms.example: The job is physically demanding and requires a lot of stamina.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to the body or material things

wordForms.definition: in a realistic way; almost; nearly
wordForms.example: The project is practically finished — just a few details remain.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to real situations; useful and effective

wordForms.definition: in a way that is normal or expected
wordForms.example: Meetings typically last around forty-five minutes.
wordForms.rootDefinition: having the usual features or qualities of a type

wordForms.definition: in a way that follows clear, sensible reasoning
wordForms.example: She argued her case logically and persuasively.

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The number of visitors to the site has ___ increased this year.
The country was ___ dependent on oil exports.
___, the meeting should take about an hour.
The speech was ___ charged and moved the entire audience.
She ___ thought through every step before presenting her plan.
The job is ___ exhausting, requiring long hours on your feet.

transformExercises.sentence: The situation changed ___ when the new director arrived. (DRAMATIC)
The country recovered ___ more quickly than expected. (ECONOMIC)
The project is ___ complete — only the report remains. (PRACTICE)
Prices ___ rise during the holiday season. (TYPICAL)
